title: Song
content:
She sat and sang alway
        By the green margin of a stream,
    Watching the fishes leap and play
        Beneath the glad sunbeam.

    I sat and wept alway
        Beneath the moon's most shadowy beam,
    Watching the blossoms of the May
        Weep leaves into the stream.

    I wept for memory;
        She sang for hope that is so fair:
    My tears were swallowed by the sea;
        Her songs died on the air.
meaning: About: Christina Georgina Rossetti was a British poet, who wrote a variety of romantic, devotional, and children's poems. She is best known for her long poem Goblin Market, her love poem "Remember", and for her Christmas poem "In the Bleak Midwinter".
